bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with the worst possible offer for me (all valueE = 0.20 utility each), giving me a total utility of only 0.20. This is clearly a low-ball opening. I'm at t=0.00 so I should counter with my ideal offer (all valueA) which gives me maximum utility of 1.0. There's no reason to concede at all this early.

It's very early (t=0.02) and the opponent started with all valueE (worst for me) and has only slightly moved issueA to valueD. I should stay ambitious and make a small concession from my opening bid. I'll concede slightly on lower-weight issues (issueD, issueE) while holding firm on high-weight issues (issueA, issueB). My utility: issueA=valueA(0.3), issueB=valueA(0.3), issueC=valueA(0.2), issueD=valueB(0.08), issueE=valueB(0.08) = 0.96.

The opponent is very slowly conceding on issueA (from valueE to valueD to valueC) while holding firm at valueE on all other issues. This is a very gradual concession pattern. We're only at t=0.04, so there's no urgency to concede heavily. I'll make a small concession by moving issueC down slightly (from valueA to valueB) while holding firm on issueA and issueB which have the highest importance weights. I'll keep issueD and issueE at valueB as before since they're low-weight issues I already conceded on.

My best response is to continue pushing for high utility on my most important issues (A and B, weight 0.3 each) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. I'll keep issueA and issueB at valueA, slightly concede on issueC, and give a bit more on D and E. We're still very early (t=0.06), so I should stay close to my ideal bid.

The opponent has been conceding on issueA (valueE → valueD → valueC → valueB → valueA) but holding firm on issueB-E at valueE. My current utility from their latest offer: issueA=1.0*0.3 + issueB=0.2*0.3 + issueC=0.2*0.2 + issueD=0.2*0.1 + issueE=0.2*0.1 = 0.3+0.06+0.04+0.02+0.02 = 0.44. We're still very early (t=0.08), so I should hold firm on my most important issues (A and B) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. I'll keep issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA (my top priorities), and concede slightly more on issueC, issueD, issueE.

The opponent is slowly conceding on issueA (now at valueA) and issueB (moved to valueD), but still holding firm on issueC, issueD, issueE at valueE. We're at t=0.10, still very early. My highest weight issues are A and B (0.3 each), followed by C (0.2). I should maintain strong positions on A and B while making modest concessions on the lower-weighted issues (D and E).
